front: Poem: begins "I, too, dislike it" and calls for "imaginary gardens with real toads in them"
Poem: invokes "literalists of the imagination" as the "autocrats among us"
Poem: lists "the baseball fan" and "the immovable critic" among phenomena we do not admire because we do not understand them
Poem: cut from 29 lines to just 3 in the 1967 Complete Poems, with epigraph "Omissions are not accidents"
Poem: creatures "wade through black jade" at a cliffside described as a "defiant edifice" with "all the physical features of ac-cident"
Poem: set in a town with "eight stranded whales" where C. J. Poole puts up a "Danger" sign; "Durer would have seen a reason for living" there
Poem: inspired by Mount Rainier; begins "of ice. Deceptively reserved and flat" and uses a glacier as a metaphor for the title creature
Poem: describes the title creature as "a near artichoke with head and legs" and a "night miniature artist engineer" compared to "Leonardo da Vinci's replica"
Poem: a woman says "I should like to be alone" and a man replies "why not be alone together?" — ends with "the book on the writing table; the hand in the breast-pocket"
Author: edited The Dial magazine in the 1920s, where she rejected Hart Crane's work and transformed his "The Wine Menagerie" into unrecognizable free verse
Author: collaborated with Muhammad Ali on "Poem on the Annihilation of Ernie Terrell" and praised him as the "smiling pugilist"
Author: proposed car names like "Mongoose Civique" and "Utopian Turtletop" for Ford
